name: Arizona SNAP (Nutrition Assistance)
description: Arizona's SNAP program provides monthly food benefits on an EBT card called the Quest card. Benefits can be used to purchase groceries at authorized retailers and some farmers markets.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 185% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: Arizona Health Care Cost Containment System (AHCCCS/Medicaid)
description: Arizona's Medicaid program providing comprehensive health coverage to eligible residents. Covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, behavioral health, dental, and vision services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant individuals, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL; children up to 200% FPL

name: Arizona Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Benefits are paid weekly based on previous earnings for up to 26 weeks.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own with sufficient work history
incomeLimitNotes: Weekly benefit amount based on wages earned; maximum weekly benefit applies

name: Arizona Department of Housing
description: Administers rental assistance, homelessness prevention, and affordable housing programs across Arizona. Provides funding to local agencies for emergency shelter, transitional housing, and Section 8 vouchers.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families seeking affordable housing or facing homelessness
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally targets households at or below 80% of area median income

name: Community Legal Services of Arizona
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Arizonans in areas including housing, family law, consumer protection, public benefits, and immigration. Serves Maricopa, Mohave, Yavapai, La Paz, and Coconino counties.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in Arizona with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Southern Arizona Legal Aid (SALA)
description: Provides free legal representation to low-income residents in southern Arizona. Handles cases involving housing, domestic violence, public benefits, consumer issues, and elder law.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals in southern Arizona with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Arizona@Work
description: Arizona's statewide workforce development system providing free job search assistance, resume writing, career counseling, training programs, and employer connections through local American Job Centers.
eligibilitySummary: All Arizona job seekers and employers

name: Arizona LIHEAP - Energy Assistance
description: The Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program helps eligible low-income households pay energy bills. Provides assistance with heating and cooling costs through community action agencies.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households at or below 150% of the federal poverty level
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% FPL

name: Arizona 211
description: Free, confidential service connecting Arizonans to local community resources for food, housing, health care, utilities, and other essential services. Available 24/7 by phone and online.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Arizona seeking information about community resources
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: St. Mary's Food Bank Alliance
description: One of the largest food banks in the country, serving Maricopa and surrounding counties. Distributes food through community food pantries, mobile pantries, and direct distribution programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in central Arizona

name: Community Food Bank of Southern Arizona
description: A Feeding America partner food bank serving five counties in southern Arizona. Distributes food through a network of over 350 partner agencies including food pantries, soup kitchens, and shelters.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in southern Arizona

name: Arizona Department of Veterans' Services
description: Serves Arizona veterans and their families with benefits counseling, claims assistance, employment support, and access to state veterans' homes. Helps connect veterans to federal and state benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Arizona veterans and their dependents

name: Arizona Crisis Line (Solari Crisis & Human Services)
description: Provides 24/7 crisis intervention, counseling, and referral services for anyone experiencing a behavioral health emergency. Connects callers with mobile crisis teams and crisis stabilization services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Arizona experiencing a mental health or substance use crisis
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Arizona Child Care Assistance (DES)
description: Helps low-income families pay for child care so parents can work or attend school. Provides subsidies to licensed child care providers and certified family homes.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children under 13
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 165% of the federal poverty level

name: Arizona TANF - Cash Assistance
description: Provides temporary cash benefits to low-income families with children through the TANF program. Includes requirements for work participation and time limits on benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children under 18
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income limits; varies by family size

name: Arizona WIC Program
description: Provides nutritious foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals for pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant or postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of the federal poverty level

name: Arizona KidsCare (CHIP)
description: Arizona's Children's Health Insurance Program providing low-cost health coverage for uninsured children in families that earn too much for Medicaid but cannot afford private insurance.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 in families with income between 138-200% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Family income between 138% and 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Valley Metro Regional Public Transportation Authority
description: Operates bus and light rail service in the Phoenix metropolitan area. Offers reduced fare programs for seniors, people with disabilities, youth, and Medicare card holders.
eligibilitySummary: Phoenix metro area residents; reduced fares for seniors, disabled, youth, and Medicare holders
hours: Service hours vary by route; customer service Mon-Fri 5:00 AM - 8:00 PM

name: City of Phoenix Human Services Department
description: Provides a range of social services to Phoenix residents including senior services, family services, Head Start, utility assistance, and homeless services through community centers across the city.
eligibilitySummary: Phoenix residents in need of social services

name: Tucson Pima Collaboration to End Homelessness
description: Coordinates homeless services in the Tucson and Pima County area including emergency shelter, rapid rehousing, and permanent supportive housing. Serves as the Continuum of Care lead agency.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness in Tucson/Pima County

name: Arizona Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free energy efficiency upgrades to low-income homes to reduce energy costs. Includes insulation, air sealing, and heating/cooling system improvements.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters at or below 200% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Arizona Early Intervention Program (AzEIP)
description: Provides support and services for families with children birth to age three who have developmental delays or disabilities. Services are provided in natural environments like the home.
eligibilitySummary: Children birth to 3 years old with developmental delays or established conditions

name: 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline - Arizona
description: Provides free, confidential support for anyone in suicidal crisis or emotional distress. Call or text 988 to connect with a trained counselor 24/7.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing suicidal thoughts, mental health crisis, or emotional distress
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services in 150+ languages

name: Arizona Rehabilitation Services Administration
description: Provides vocational rehabilitation services to help people with disabilities prepare for, obtain, and maintain employment. Services include job training, job placement, and assistive technology.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Arizona Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Provides crisis support, safety planning, and referrals for survivors of domestic violence. Connects callers with shelter, legal advocacy, and support services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing domestic violence, dating violence, or sexual assault
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Arizona Financial Aid - AZ Grants
description: State financial aid programs for Arizona residents attending eligible in-state colleges and universities. Multiple grant and scholarship programs to support postsecondary education.
eligibilitySummary: Arizona residents attending eligible in-state postsecondary institutions

name: Phoenix Community Action Program (CAP)
description: Provides essential services to low-income Phoenix residents including utility assistance, eviction prevention, emergency food boxes, and senior services. Helps families achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Phoenix residents at or below 200% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Sun Tran - Tucson Public Transit
description: Operates bus service in the Tucson metropolitan area with reduced fare programs for seniors, people with disabilities, youth, and Medicare card holders. Sun Van provides paratransit service.
eligibilitySummary: Tucson area residents; reduced fares for seniors, disabled, youth, and Medicare holders
hours: Service varies by route; customer service Mon-Fri 6:00 AM - 7:00 PM

name: Arizona Prescription Assistance Program
description: Helps uninsured and underinsured Arizonans access affordable prescription medications. Provides information about pharmaceutical company patient assistance programs and discount programs.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured or underinsured Arizona residents who need help affording prescriptions

name: Arizona Department of Education - Adult Education
description: Provides adult basic education, GED preparation, English language learning, and workforce readiness classes at locations across Arizona. Free programs for eligible adults.
eligibilitySummary: Adults 16+ who are not enrolled in school and need basic education, GED, or English language skills
